reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce a solution: disable compression in sshd_config.

The finding: sshd_disable_compression: need to set Compression no (or delayed). According to description: "To disable compression or delay compression until after a user has successfully authenticated, add or correct the following line in the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file: Compression". So we need to ensure line "Compression no" exists in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.

We need to use run_cmd, read_file, write_file. Approach: read file, check if line exists (maybe commented). If not present or commented, we need to ensure line present uncommented with value "no". Could also set to "delayed". But rule likely expects "no". We'll set "Compression no". If line exists commented (# Compression ...) we need to uncomment and set value.

Simplify: read entire file, replace any line matching ^\s*#?\s*Compression\s+.* with "Compression no". Ensure exactly one line. Write back.

We'll use read_file to get content, then use sed via run_cmd? But we can't use sed directly? We can use run_cmd to run sed. But we need to modify file in-place. Could use run_cmd with sed -i.

Simpler: read file content, process in our logic (but we don't have string manipulation functions). We only have tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file. We can read file, then write modified content using write_file. To modify content we need to do it ourselves? We cannot manipulate strings directly. But we can use run_cmd to call sed and output to a temp file then write_file? But we can just run_cmd with sed -i to edit file directly. That's allowed: we use run_cmd to execute a shell command. So we can do:

run_cmd: sed -i -E 's/^\s*#?\s*Compression\s+.*/Compression no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

But need to ensure line exists; if not, sed won't add. So we can also append if not found. Could do: run_cmd: grep -q '^\s*#?\s*Compression\s' /etc/ssh/sshd_config || echo 'Compression no' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

But that may add duplicate if line commented? Better: we can first replace any existing line (commented or not) then if no replacement occurred, append.

Simpler: we can delete all lines matching Compression then add line. Use sed -i '/^\s*#?\s*Compression\s/d' then echo.

Thus:

run_cmd: sed -i '/^\s*#?\s*Compression\s/d' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
run_cmd: echo 'Compression no' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

That ensures exactly one line at end.

Potential issue: ordering not important.
